description: E2E test coverage improvements — ledger-state assertions and full CLI option coverage across payment, trust, account set, and offer commands

userStories.title: Payment: balance verification + memo-type/format + key-material tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want payment tests to verify XRP and IOU balance changes and to cover --memo-type, --memo-format, --mnemonic, and --account/--keystore/--password.
acceptanceCriteria: In payment.core.test.ts, the 'sends 1 XRP' test queries sender balance before and after via 'account info --json' and asserts it decreased by at least 1 XRP (1000000 drops); asserts recipient balance increased by exactly 1000000 drops
In payment.iou.test.ts, after the IOU payment query the recipient's trust line via 'account trust-lines --json <address>' and assert the balance field reflects the received amount

Add test for --memo-type and --memo-format: run 'payment send ... --memo-type text --memo-format text --dry-run'; parse tx JSON and assert Memos[0].Memo.MemoType and MemoFormat are present (hex-encoded strings)
Add test for --mnemonic key material: fund a wallet derived from a known mnemonic via fundFromFaucet, run 'payment send ... --mnemonic <mnemonic> --node testnet', assert exit 0

Add test for --account/--keystore/--password key material: create a keystore entry in a temp dir via 'wallet import', run 'payment send ... --account <addr> --keystore <dir> --password <pw>', assert exit 0
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Payment: --no-ripple-direct, --limit-quality flags + --partial/--deliver-min delivered amount
userStories.description: As a developer, I want routing flag options tested via --dry-run Flags inspection, and partial payment tests to verify the actual delivered amount.
acceptanceCriteria: Add test for --no-ripple-direct: run 'payment send ... --no-ripple-direct --dry-run --json'; parse tx JSON and assert (Flags & 0x00040000) !== 0 (tfNoRippleDirect bit set)
Add test for --limit-quality: run 'payment send ... --limit-quality --dry-run --json'; parse tx JSON and assert (Flags & 0x00080000) !== 0 (tfLimitQuality bit set)
In payment.paths.test.ts, the '--partial --deliver-min' test must run with '--json' and assert meta.delivered_amount is a string representing a value >= the deliver-min amount and > 0
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Trust set: on-chain trust-line verification + --mnemonic coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want trust set tests to verify the trust line actually appears on-chain via account trust-lines query, and to cover the --mnemonic key-material path.
acceptanceCriteria: In trust.core.test.ts, the 'creates USD trust line' test queries 'account trust-lines --json <address>' after the CLI call and asserts a trust line with currency 'USD' and the correct issuer address exists in the result
In trust.core.test.ts, the 'alias s' test similarly asserts the trust line exists after the command runs
In trust.core.test.ts, the '--account/--keystore/--password' test asserts the trust line exists after the command runs

Add a new test for --mnemonic: fund a wallet derived from a known mnemonic, run 'trust set ... --mnemonic <mnemonic>', assert exit 0 and query 'account trust-lines --json' to verify the trust line was created
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Account set: --email-hash, --transfer-rate, --tick-size with on-chain verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for account set fields that query account info and verify the ledger reflects each change.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/account/account.set.fields.test.ts with its own beforeAll that funds one wallet via fundFromFaucet
Test --email-hash: run 'account set --email-hash <32-char-hex-string> --seed <seed> --node testnet'; query 'account info --json <address>' and assert account_data.EmailHash equals the submitted value
Test --transfer-rate: run 'account set --transfer-rate 1005000000 --seed <seed> --node testnet'; query 'account info --json <address>' and assert account_data.TransferRate === 1005000000
Test --tick-size: run 'account set --tick-size 5 --seed <seed> --node testnet'; query 'account info --json <address>' and assert account_data.TickSize === 5
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Account set: --set-flag/--clear-flag on-chain verification + --mnemonic/--account key-material
userStories.description: As a developer, I want --set-flag to be verified on-chain, --clear-flag to be tested, and --mnemonic/--account key-material paths to be covered.
acceptanceCriteria: Add test for --set-flag: run 'account set --set-flag 8 --seed <seed> --node testnet' (asfDefaultRipple=8); query 'account info --json <address>' and assert (account_data.Flags & 0x00800000) !== 0

Add test for --clear-flag: set the flag first, then run 'account set --clear-flag 8 --seed <seed> --node testnet'; query 'account info --json <address>' and assert (account_data.Flags & 0x00800000) === 0
Add test for --mnemonic: run 'account set --domain example.com --mnemonic <mnemonic> --node testnet', assert exit 0
Add test for --account/--keystore/--password: run 'account set --domain example2.com --account <addr> --keystore <dir> --password <pw> --node testnet', assert exit 0
New tests should go in account.set.fields.test.ts (from US-004) or a new account.set.flags.test.ts if a separate beforeAll is cleaner
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Offer: cancel output modes + --fill-or-kill + key-material coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want offer cancel output modes (--json, --dry-run, --no-wait), offer create --fill-or-kill, and key-material paths (--mnemonic, --account) to be covered by tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Add test for 'offer cancel --json': pre-create an offer via xrpl.js, cancel with '--json', assert stdout parses as JSON with hash (64-char hex) and result 'tesSUCCESS'
Add test for 'offer cancel --dry-run': assert stdout parses as JSON with tx.TransactionType === 'OfferCancel' and tx_blob string; assert no change in account offers count
Add test for 'offer cancel --no-wait': assert exit 0 and stdout matches /[0-9A-Fa-f]{64}/

Add test for 'offer create --fill-or-kill': run offer create with --fill-or-kill; assert exit 0 (tecKILLED is non-fatal); assert the offer is NOT present in 'account offers --json' (FOK offers that cannot be fully filled are not placed)
Add test for 'offer create --mnemonic': fund a wallet derived from a known mnemonic, run offer create with --mnemonic, assert exit 0
Add test for 'offer create --account/--keystore/--password': create keystore entry, run offer create with --account/--keystore/--password, assert exit 0
Tests should be added to existing offer.core.test.ts or offer.flags.test.ts as appropriate; create a new file only if a separate beforeAll is needed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
